data.title: Tech Voices: Nvidia's H200 chips, Coinbase eyes prediction markets
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact markets, sectors, or individual stocks.

  • Nvidia (NVDA) is reportedly eyeing boosting production capacity for its H200 AI chips due to strong demand for the product in China.

Leading Chinese tech companies such as Alibaba (BABA) and ByteDance (BDNCE) have already reached out to Nvidia about buying the chips and are expected to place large orders. The Chinese government, however, has not yet decided whether it will allow the chips to be shipped to China, Reuters reported, citing sources close to the matter.

Reuters noted that Nvidia has limited quantities of H200 chips in production as it has been focusing on its new Blackwell and Rubin chips.

The Trump administration greenlighted sales of H200 chips to China earlier this week, ending an export ban that had been implemented over national security concerns. In return, Nvidia is expected to give the U.S. government 25% of the proceeds generated by sales of the chips.

  • Coinbase (COIN) is planning to launch products for prediction markets and tokenized equities.

The company will unveil the products on Dec. 17. Its tokenized equities will be launched in-house and not through a partner, according to Bloomberg, which cited a source close to the matter.

Bloomberg noted that Robinhood (HOOD) began offering Kalshi prediction market products earlier this year, while both Robinhood and Kraken (KRAKEN) offer tokenized U.S. stocks and ETFs outside the U.S. Crypto.com and Trump Media (DJT) have also partnered to offer prediction market products.

data.title: Bristol Myers Squibb upgraded at Guggenheim on 2026 pipeline catalysts
data.description:
• Guggenheim upgraded Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) to buy from neutral saying that results for several pipeline candidates by the end of 2026 could lead to upside. 
  • The firm set a price target of $62 (21% upside based on Dec. 11 close). 
  • Analyst Seamus Fernandez highlighted two assets in particular: the oral Factor XIa inhibitor milvexian for stroke prevention in atrial fibrillation and secondary stroke prevention; and admilparant, an LPA1 antagonist, for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis and progressive pulmonary fibrosis.
  • In 2026, phase 3 results for milvexian in both indications are expected, as well as for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis for admilparant.
  • Fernandez noted that positive trial results for Bayer's (BAYZF)(BAYRY) own Factor XIa inhibitor, asundexian, bode well for milvexian. As a result, he is boosting his 2033 global sales estimate for milvexian to $6B from $2.2B.
  • He sees 2033 sales of admilparant of $2.5B, up from negligible prior.
  • Fernandez also sees Bristol benefitting from growth in royalties for pulmonary arterial hypertension treatment Winrevair (sotatercept), which is marketed by Merck (MRK).

data.title: Thinking Machines announces general availability of API
data.description:
• Thinking Machines, the artificial intelligence startup founded by former OpenAI (OPENAI) CTO Mira Murati, announced the general availability of its application programming interface on Friday.
  • Known as Tinker, the API supports models from a wide variety of companies, including Meta's (META) Llama, Alibaba's (BABA) Qwen, OpenAI's GPT-OSS and DeepSeek's (DEEPSEEK) V3.1.
  • Pricing for the API varies by model, according to the company's website.
  • Separately, on Friday, it added support for Moonshot's Kimi-2-Thinking model. “With a trillion parameters, Kimi K2 is the largest model in our lineup so far. It is built for long chains of reasoning and tool use,” Thinking Machines wrote on its website.
  • In July, Thinking Machines raised $2B in a funding round that valued it at $10B. Andreessen Horowitz, also known as a16z, led the funding round, which included participation by Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), ServiceNow (NOW), Cisco Systems (CSCO) and others.

data.title: STAAR Surgical falls as proxy adviser recommends against revised Alcon deal (update)
data.description:
Update 2:30pm: Adds STAAR Surgical comment.

STAAR Surgical (STAA) dropped 5% after proxy adviser Glass Lewis recommended holders reject the revised $30.75 a share offer from Alcon (ALC).

“We do not believe there exists persuasive cause for investors to endorse the revised Alcon arrangement," Glass Lewis wrote in its report, according to a statement from Broadwood Partners, STAAR's biggest shareholder, who has been opposed to the sale. 

The latest recommendation comes after Alcon (ALC) on Tuesday  raised its offer to $30.75 from the original deal price of $28. Shareholders are scheduled to vote on the transaction on Dec. 19. 

“In reaffirming its opposition to this misbegotten transaction, Glass Lewis correctly concluded that the revised price is not compelling and that the STAAR Board lacks credibility," Neal C. Brasher, Broadwood founder and president, said in the statement. 

Broadwood, which has a 30% stake in STAAR (STAA), and holder Yunqi Capital both earlier this week said they still plan to vote against the Alcon  deal even with the revised bid.

“We stand by the comprehensive sales process overseen by a Board committed to maximizing value for STAAR stockholder," STAAR said in a statement to Seeking Alpha.  "Since the initial announcement of the Alcon merger agreement, Broadwood has distorted the truth and worked at every turn to derail the transaction to serve its own interests. STAAR believes its stockholders will be harmed if this transaction is not approved and that the value of STAAR will be significantly less. STAAR stockholders have a choice: $30.75 in cash from Alcon or allow Broadwood to obtain even greater influence without paying any premium to other stockholders.”

data.title: J&J's Akeega granted expanded indication in prostate cancer
data.description:
• The U.S. FDA has expanded the indication for Johnson & Johnson's (JNJ) Akeega (niraparib and abiraterone) to include BRCA2-mutated metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer.
  • The approval was based on results from the AMPLITUDE trial that showed statistically significant improvement in radiographic progression-free survival for those on Akeega plus prednisone compared to those on placebo and abiraterone.
  • Akeega was already approved for BRCA-mutated metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer.

data.title: Nasdaq slides more than 1% as Broadcom guidance concerns pressures AI trade
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were lower on Friday, with the Nasdaq Composite retracting the most as the AI trade came under pressure dragging the index.

The teach-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last -1.5% in afternoon trade after paring some loses, while the S&P 500 (SP500) was -1%. 

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) had continued making new highs reaching a top of 48,886.86 points, but retrieved and was last -0.5%.

Chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO) -11.2% warned of margin pressures and disappointed investors with a lesser-than-expected backlog figure for AI product orders. 

“The bull is just three years old, there’s still a massive Wall of Worry to climb, there’s plenty of cash on the sidelines, corporate earnings are better than analysts expected, the consumer is not as dire as many proclaimed, and the Fed is easing – not tightening. Plus, many sectors and stocks are well rested and ready to run,” said Robert Edwards, CIO at Edwards Asset Management.

“What a run the mega-cap growth shares have been on – impressive. But we think 2026 is the year the leadership broadens and the baton is passed. Mega-cap growth has carried the load, but about two handfuls of companies now represent nearly 40% of the index. It’s time to spread the money around. Dividend growers, value, industrials, smaller companies – all have been forgotten, and forgotten stocks, whose operations are strong, often provide the biggest surprise,” Edwards added.

Also, among the Nasdaq’s ((COMP:IND)) top decliners are Micron Technology (MU), Applovin (APP), Palantir (PLTR), and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD).

On the economic side, Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee said that waiting on more data before lowering interest rates would have been more prudent. Also, Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack said she would prefer a “slightly more” restrictive monetary policy.

“The Federal Reserve is solidly in the rate cutting mode, and while they aren’t racing to cut, they’re clearly in the rate cutting lane. Powell noted this week, following their rate cut decision, that the jobs market may be weaker than reported – that gives the Fed cover to keep easing. And with a new Trump-selected Fed Chair coming in May, investors should be preparing for an even more dovish and  accommodative Fed,” said Edwards.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.20%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was 1 basis point lower to 3.54%.

data.title: PNC bags regulatory approvals for FirstBank acquisition
data.description:
PNC Financial Services Group (PNC) on Friday announced the receipt of regulatory approvals for the ~$4.1B acquisition of FirstBank Holding.

The company said the approvals have been received from the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, and the Colorado Division of Banking.

The transaction is expected to close on or about January 5.

data.title: LENZ plunges after adverse event report on eye therapy
data.description:
Shares of LENZ Therapeutics (LENZ) fell ~26% on Friday, a move TD Cowen attributed to investor worries over an adverse event reported on a federal database about the company’s newly launched eye therapy Vizz (aceclidine), Bloomberg News reported.

According to analyst Stacy Ku, who has a Buy rating on LENZ, the FDA’s adverse-event database, known as FAERS, indicated a retinal tear linked to Vizz, which the agency approved in July for the treatment of presbyopia (blurry near vision) in adults.

“The perceived safety overhang from retinal detachment ((RD)) risk impacted the Vuity launch (beyond lack of efficacy).” Ku said, referring to a rival presbyopia therapy from AbbVie (ABBV), and adding that’s why leading physicians “do retinal exams ahead of prescribing VIZZ.”

However, the analyst noted the incidence so far is consistent with LENZ’s (LENZ) guidance. Citing the company management, she said that the patient in question, who had a history of retinal disease and hadn’t received a retinal exam, is “recovering well” following the tear in the right eye.

data.title: Lotus debuts its first plug-in hybrid as its sports car legacy enters a new era
data.description:
Lotus Technology (LOT) debuted its first-ever plug-in hybrid version of the Eletre SUV this week. The PHEV is part of the company's broader "Super Hybrid" strategy aimed at boosting sales and widening appeal in key markets such as China and Europe. 

The vehicle pairs a 2.0-liter turbocharged gasoline engine with a powerful electric system, delivering headline outputs of up to about 952 hp, rapid 0–60 mph performance of around 3 seconds, and a reported total range exceeding 600 miles depending on the battery configuration.

The PHEV model marks a notable strategic adjustment from Lotus' (LOT) earlier plan to become an EV-only brand by around 2028, reflecting softer-than-expected demand for all-electric vehicles and the commercial need for more flexible electrified offerings, while still sitting alongside fully electric products like the Evija hypercar and the Emeya sedan and future EV sports cars such as the planned Type 135. 

Lotus through the years 

Lotus was founded by Colin Chapman in 1952 as a small British sports car and racing constructor, initially operating independently and focused on kit cars and lightweight racers. Through the 1950s–1970s, Lotus Cars and Team Lotus achieved success in Formula 1 and built road cars like the Elite, Elan, and Esprit. In 1986, General Motors (GM) acquired a majority stake in Lotus. In 1993, Italian businessman Romano Artioli’s Bugatti-linked interests bought Lotus from GM (GM). By 1996, Malaysia’s national carmaker Proton (through DRB-HICOM) took control of Lotus. Finally, Chinese auto giant Geely agreed to buy a controlling stake in Lotus in May 2017.

data.title: Coinbase set to launch prediction markets, tokenized equities on December 17 - report
data.description:
Article was revised at 13.53 PM ET to include information from CNBC report.

Coinbase Global (COIN) is set to launch prediction markets and tokenized equities at a showcase on December 17, Bloomberg News reported, citing a person familiar with the matter.

Tokenized stocks are expected to be launched in-house, according to the person who asked not to be named because the information wasn't yet public.

The new products' rollout are a part of the company's push to become an everything app, noted Bloomberg.

"Tune in to the livestream on Dec. 17 to find out what new products Coinbase is shipping," a Coinbase spokesperson reportedly told the news outlet.

Yesterday, Kalshi (KALSHI) and the crypto exchange company Crypto.com announced a coalition for prediction markets, which includes Coinbase (COIN), Robinhood (HOOD), and the sports gaming platform Underdog.

Separately, a source close to the matter told CNBC News that there could be a formal announcement for the Kalshi-powered in-house prediction market by Coinbase as early as next week.

The deal also underscores Kalshi's push to embed its event contracts into various brokerages, the CNBC report noted.
